proposed_from.topic: Sharp partial identification of principal-stratum-specific average causal effects when the post-treatment intermediate variable is ordinal with K>=3 levels and the principal ignorability assumption is relaxed to a bounded covariate-conditional principal-score divergence between adjacent stratum pairs, recovering Frangakis-Rubin point identification under principal ignorability and the binary case at the trivial bound
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"The closest repo analogue is the banked proposal pid_ordinal_late_partial_defiers_v1 Conjecture 1, which also uses a shared ordinal middle object to show pairwise binary relaxations can be too wide. The epsilon-gap is that pid_ordinal_late_partial_defiers_v1 couples full ordinal IV response paths such as 010 across adjacent instrument rungs, whereas this proposal couples posterior principal-stratum weights q_{z,g}(x,y) inside observed (Z,S,X,Y) cells through adjacent principal-score divergence and calibration to a supplied ordinal principal-score vector π. Thus the common mechanism is ordinal shared-middle compatibility, but the theorem-level object here is a principal-stratum posterior-chain sensitivity LP, not an ordinal IV bounded-defier response-path interval.","published_axis":"DingLu2017 and JiangYangDing2022 cover principal-score point identification under principal ignorability, which this proposal treats only as Theorem 1's Γ=1 calibration corner. NguyenStuartScharfsteinOgburn2024 and DornGuoKallus2022 are closest for binary sensitivity geometry, while WuAntonelli2024 and TongKahanHarhayLi2025 are closest for PI violations and odds-ratio sensitivity, but none isolates a finite K>=3 ordinal adjacency graph whose shared middle stratum creates a sharp nonrectangular posterior-chain compatibility polytope."}
seed_list: Prior work DingLu2017 would imply that principal-score PI formulas are the natural baseline for latent principal strata. Our seed conjectures that for ordinal K>=3 intermediates, bounded adjacent posterior divergence yields a chain-coupled sharp LP and binary principal-score splits are strictly nonsharp because a shared middle stratum must use one compatible tilt.

Prior work WuAntonelli2024 establishes that PI violations create partial ID even when principal-strata distributions are known. Our seed conjectures a sharper phase diagram, because adjacent ordinal divergence gives PI point ID at Gamma=1, a finite chain LP for 1<Gamma<infty, and calibrated Manski cell bounds at Gamma=infty.

Prior work NguyenStuartScharfsteinOgburn2024 would predict that PI sensitivity can be organized by binary complier/noncomplier odds-ratio perturbations. Our seed conjectures that K>=3 ordinal intermediates break binary modularity because adjacent pair analyses choose incompatible tilts for the shared middle level.

Prior work TongKahanHarhayLi2025 assumes an odds-ratio sensitivity parameter can support semiparametric principal-strata analysis beyond monotonicity. Our seed conjectures that the finite ordinal case has a sharper nonparametric object: a calibrated adjacent-chain posterior polytope whose extreme points are not captured by marginal odds-ratio summaries alone.

Prior work LuJiangDing2024 treats non-binary post-treatment variables through continuous principal-strata surfaces. Our seed conjectures that finite ordinal strata are not just a discretized continuous problem, because the K=3 adjacency graph already creates a minimal nonrectangular sharpness gap.

Prior work FrauenMelnychukFeuerriegel2023 establishes generalized sharp sensitivity bounds as distribution shifts for causal effects. Our seed conjectures that latent principal-stratum posterior divergence is not reducible to treatment-confounding distribution shift because principal-score calibration must hold inside observed ordinal cells.

Prior work DornGuoKallus2022 would suggest sharp bounded-odds sensitivity can be solved by binary posterior tilting. Our seed conjectures that applying doubly-sharp binary tilts to every adjacent ordinal split is nonsharp because one latent posterior vector must satisfy all adjacent inequalities simultaneously.

Prior work MatteiMealli2011 shows augmented designs can sharpen principal-strata direct-effect bounds. Our seed conjectures that even without augmented randomization, ordinal adjacency plus bounded posterior divergence can sharpen over dichotomized analyses because the ordinal graph imposes compatibility equations.

Prior work LeeDanielsSargent2010 models ordinal QOL outcomes under principal stratification with sensitivity assumptions. Our seed conjectures that dichotomizing ordinal QOL severity before PI sensitivity loses sharpness because threshold-specific binary analyses ignore cross-threshold posterior calibration.

Prior work WangZhangMealliBornkamp2023 uses multiple-imputation sensitivity analyses for PI in clinical estimands. Our seed conjectures that a finite adjacent-chain LP provides the sharp benchmark those MI analyses should be checked against because imputed stratum memberships must satisfy one calibrated posterior law.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work DingLu2017 would imply that principal-score PI formulas are the natural baseline for latent principal strata. Our seed conjectures that for ordinal K>=3 intermediates, bounded adjacent posterior divergence yields a chain-coupled sharp LP and binary principal-score splits are strictly nonsharp because a shared middle stratum must use one compatible tilt.

anchor_paper.author: Ding and Lu
anchor_paper.claim: Principal scores support nonparametric identification of principal causal effects under principal ignorability and related assumptions.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime-shift pattern, not blocked: K>=3 is used to expose a strict binary-shortcut failure, not merely to extend binary notation.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from DingLu2017 plus standard binary sensitivity bounds because ordinal levels can be dichotomized.
seed_details.rebuttal: False if the same middle stratum appears in two adjacent splits; dichotomized optimizers need not correspond to one calibrated posterior vector.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work WuAntonelli2024 establishes that PI violations create partial ID even when principal-strata distributions are known. Our seed conjectures a sharper phase diagram, because adjacent ordinal divergence gives PI point ID at Gamma=1, a finite chain LP for 1<Gamma<infty, and calibrated Manski cell bounds at Gamma=infty.

anchor_paper.author: Wu and Antonelli
anchor_paper.claim: Even known principal-strata distributions do not point-identify principal causal effects when principal ignorability is violated.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Sharpens a named claim by adding a regime boundary and sharp finite program; not just weakening PI.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from WuAntonelli2024 because they already study weaker assumptions after PI fails.
seed_details.rebuttal: Their abstract partial-ID warning does not identify the ordinal adjacent-chain geometry or binary-shortcut gap.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work NguyenStuartScharfsteinOgburn2024 would predict that PI sensitivity can be organized by binary complier/noncomplier odds-ratio perturbations. Our seed conjectures that K>=3 ordinal intermediates break binary modularity because adjacent pair analyses choose incompatible tilts for the shared middle level.

anchor_paper.author: Nguyen, Stuart, Scharfstein, and Ogburn
anchor_paper.claim: PI violations in one-sided noncompliance can be assessed by sensitivity parameters including odds ratios for compliers and noncompliers.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends binary to ordinal but not blocked because the claimed phenomenon is binary modularity failure and strict nonsharpness.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is only the obvious multi-category version of their binary sensitivity analysis.
seed_details.rebuttal: The ordinal proposal predicts a new compatibility obstruction absent from a single binary pair.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work TongKahanHarhayLi2025 assumes an odds-ratio sensitivity parameter can support semiparametric principal-strata analysis beyond monotonicity. Our seed conjectures that the finite ordinal case has a sharper nonparametric object: a calibrated adjacent-chain posterior polytope whose extreme points are not captured by marginal odds-ratio summaries alone.

anchor_paper.author: Tong, Kahan, Harhay, and Li
anchor_paper.claim: A margin-free conditional odds-ratio sensitivity parameter supports semiparametric principal-stratification analysis beyond monotonicity.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Mechanism decomposition, not blocked: distinguishes semiparametric odds-ratio modeling from finite sharp identified-set geometry.
seed_details.referee_objection: Their odds-ratio framework already covers this sensitivity model.
seed_details.rebuttal: Coverage of a parameter is not a sharp finite-support theorem or a proof of binary dichotomization failure.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work LuJiangDing2024 treats non-binary post-treatment variables through continuous principal-strata surfaces. Our seed conjectures that finite ordinal strata are not just a discretized continuous problem, because the K=3 adjacency graph already creates a minimal nonrectangular sharpness gap.

anchor_paper.author: Lu, Jiang, and Ding
anchor_paper.claim: Continuous post-treatment variables require new nonparametric identification and semiparametric estimation theory for principal stratification.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime-shift pattern, not blocked: finite ordinal K=3 is claimed to have a minimal graph obstruction, not merely fewer continuous levels.
seed_details.referee_objection: Continuous-strata theory subsumes ordinal strata by discretization.
seed_details.rebuttal: The proposed object is a finite posterior-polytope sharpness and dichotomization gap statement, not an estimand-surface approximation.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work FrauenMelnychukFeuerriegel2023 establishes generalized sharp sensitivity bounds as distribution shifts for causal effects. Our seed conjectures that latent principal-stratum posterior divergence is not reducible to treatment-confounding distribution shift because principal-score calibration must hold inside observed ordinal cells.

anchor_paper.author: Frauen, Melnychuk, and Feuerriegel
anchor_paper.claim: Generalized marginal sensitivity models yield sharp bounds for a broad class of causal effects under unobserved confounding.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counter-reduction mechanism, not blocked: the seed identifies a calibration obstruction in a named sharp-bounds workflow.
seed_details.referee_objection: Their generalized sensitivity framework already gives sharp bounds for mediation/path effects, so this is a relabeling.
seed_details.rebuttal: The latent principal-stratum posterior must match known principal-score calibration within observed S cells, a constraint not represented by ordinary treatment-propensity shifts.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work DornGuoKallus2022 would suggest sharp bounded-odds sensitivity can be solved by binary posterior tilting. Our seed conjectures that applying doubly-sharp binary tilts to every adjacent ordinal split is nonsharp because one latent posterior vector must satisfy all adjacent inequalities simultaneously.

anchor_paper.author: Dorn, Guo, and Kallus
anchor_paper.claim: Bounded-odds unmeasured-confounding sensitivity admits sharp ATE bounds via distributionally robust optimization.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extension-to-new-setting pattern, not blocked because the extension is predicted to fail as a binary shortcut.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is standard robust optimization with more categories.
seed_details.rebuttal: The proposed strict-gap witness is about incompatibility between adjacent binary optimizers, not solver scalability.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work MatteiMealli2011 shows augmented designs can sharpen principal-strata direct-effect bounds. Our seed conjectures that even without augmented randomization, ordinal adjacency plus bounded posterior divergence can sharpen over dichotomized analyses because the ordinal graph imposes compatibility equations.

anchor_paper.author: Mattei and Mealli
anchor_paper.claim: Augmented experimental designs can partially identify principal-strata direct effects and produce large-sample bounds.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Mechanism contrast, not blocked: compares design-based sharpening with ordinal-graph compatibility sharpening.
seed_details.referee_objection: Without augmented design information, there is no new identifying content beyond Manski bounds.
seed_details.rebuttal: The bounded adjacent posterior divergence is additional sensitivity information, and the novelty is its ordinal compatibility geometry.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work LeeDanielsSargent2010 models ordinal QOL outcomes under principal stratification with sensitivity assumptions. Our seed conjectures that dichotomizing ordinal QOL severity before PI sensitivity loses sharpness because threshold-specific binary analyses ignore cross-threshold posterior calibration.

anchor_paper.author: Lee, Daniels, and Sargent
anchor_paper.claim: Three-arm clinical trials with ordinal quality-of-life outcomes and death/progression can be analyzed using principal stratification and sensitivity assumptions.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Targeted applied phenomenon, not blocked: the surprise is sharpness loss from dichotomizing ordinal severity, not applying an existing method to QOL.
seed_details.referee_objection: Applied ordinal QOL sensitivity analyses already model these categories parametrically.
seed_details.rebuttal: Parametric sensitivity analysis is not a sharp nonparametric adjacent-divergence bound or a theorem showing binary threshold nonsharpness.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work WangZhangMealliBornkamp2023 uses multiple-imputation sensitivity analyses for PI in clinical estimands. Our seed conjectures that a finite adjacent-chain LP provides the sharp benchmark those MI analyses should be checked against because imputed stratum memberships must satisfy one calibrated posterior law.

anchor_paper.author: Wang, Zhang, Mealli, and Bornkamp
anchor_paper.claim: Multiple-imputation joint models can assess sensitivity to principal ignorability in clinical principal-stratum analyses.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Benchmarking phenomenon, not blocked: identifies a sharpness criterion for a named workflow rather than proposing another imputation model.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just a nonparametric version of their imputation sensitivity analysis.
seed_details.rebuttal: The LP gives sharp identified sets under explicit calibration and adjacency constraints; MI model output can be checked against it but does not imply it.

proposed_from.literature_map: FrangakisRubin2002 (Biometrics, https://faculty.washington.edu/peterg/Vaccine2006/articles/FrangakisRubin.2002.pdf): principal strata define causal effects within joint potential intermediate values; open issue is identification when strata are latent. VanderWeele2011 (IJB): principal stratification often needs bounds or sensitivity analysis; tension is usefulness versus latent-stratum assumptions. DingLu2017 (JRSSB, https://academic.oup.com/jrsssb/article/79/3/757/7040654): principal scores identify PCEs under monotonicity and PI; tension is untestable PI and binary/simple strata. JiangYangDing2022 (JRSSB, https://academic.oup.com/jrsssb/article/84/4/1423/7073277): multiply robust estimation under PI; open gap is sharp partial ID when PI fails. ForastiereMealliVanderWeele2018 (Biometrika, https://academic.oup.com/biomet/article/105/4/979/5142335): clarifies PI in mediation; tension is extrapolation versus principal-strata targets. NguyenStuartScharfsteinOgburn2024 (arXiv:2303.05032, https://arxiv.org/abs/2303.05032): sensitivity analysis for PI violation in binary one-sided noncompliance; open gap is ordinal K>=3 latent-cell geometry. WuAntonelli2024 (arXiv:2412.06628, https://arxiv.org/abs/2412.06628): PI violations imply partial ID even with known principal strata distributions; open gap is a sharp ordinal adjacent-divergence program. TongKahanHarhayLi2025 (arXiv:2501.17514, https://arxiv.org/abs/2501.17514): semiparametric principal stratification with odds-ratio sensitivity; tension is estimator framework versus finite sharp ordinal bounds. LeeDanielsSargent2010 (JASA, https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC3035160/): ordinal QOL outcomes under principal stratification and sensitivity analysis; phenomenon is applied demand for ordinal, not dichotomized, strata. LuJiangDing2024 (arXiv:2309.12425, https://arxiv.org/abs/2309.12425): continuous post-treatment principal stratification; gap is finite ordinal graph sharpness. DornGuoKallus2022 (arXiv:2112.11449, https://arxiv.org/abs/2112.11449) and FrauenMelnychukFeuerriegel2023 (NeurIPS, https://arxiv.org/abs/2305.16988): sharp sensitivity bounds under bounded confounding; tension is treatment-assignment sensitivity versus latent principal-stratum posterior sensitivity.
